The Heron slipped out of Port Saffron before the bells rang dawn. Elias Thorn held the wheel while Mira Vale watched the harbor lights shrink behind them, the recovered cargo lashed tight in the hold. The Salt Guild would send letters, and the letters would chase the Heron up the coast, and someday there would be a reckoning in some other port. Mira Vale filed the thought away with her charts. Elias Thorn set the course north, past the shoals, and Port Saffron sank back into the fog it had come from.
